69248.name: Fork in the Path
69248.description: The path forks here in three different directions, leading deep into the surrounding forest each way. The trees to the west loom before you like ancient sentinels, waiting to see which path you choose. Something catches your eye to the south.

69249.name: Old Forested Path
69249.description: Ancient gnarled pines press in on all sides, casting a deep shade across the needle covered path. Occasional birdcalls and the rustling of wild life add life to the old forest.

69250.name: Ancient Path
69250.description: The surrounding woods has an almost oppressing air, the only reprieve being the break offered by the path, allowing a sliver of sky the pierce the other wise impenetrable ceiling of needles. The trail here is barely visible, as time has allowed the forest to begin to reclaim it.

69251.name: Ancient Path's End
69251.description: The path comes to a broken end as the surrounding forest reclaims its territory. The branches above interlock in a solid curtain, casting the area in shade. The soft rustling of the forest inhabitants does what it can to add life to the otherwise total silence.

69252.name: Ancient Woods
69252.description: Great pines stand all around you and you can't help but admire these ancient trees that have weathered countless years. The matted pines crack softly beneath your feet as you walk.

69253.name: Ancient Woods
69253.description: As you continue your trek through the ancient woods, you hope you do not get lost, as there are no discernible landmarks and no perceived end to the march of pines. A tall stand of valerian sways gently, its pink flowers releasing a pungent odor.

69254.name: Ancient Woods
69254.description: Every so often a small critter darts across your path; a squirrel dashing up a tree or a fox pursuing a rabbit to its hole, but besides that, the wood is totally still. Old branches and the occasional moss covered stone are the only break in the surrounding landscape.

69255.name: Ancient Woods
69255.description: Thick, ancient pines are the only landscape for as far as you can see on all sides. The air is heavy and dense and you wonder where, if ever, these woods end.

69257.name: Off the Path
69257.description: Just off the beaten path to the north, you find yourself deep within the pine forest. Dappled light makes its way through the canopy above and various birds sing and flit in the trees. Pine needles litter the floor, disrupted only by the occasional small critter searching for food.

69258.name: A Forest Creek Bank
69258.description: A fast flowing creek cuts its way through the trees to the west. It would be foolhardy to attempt to cross here; perhaps you should look for a safer place to ford.

69262.name: Off the Path
69262.description: A small break in the trees indicates a forested path to the north and the east, and the south and west the forest stretches on endlessly. Thick trunked pine trees tower above you and cones and needles litter the ground all around you.

69264.name: Forest of Pines and Firs
69264.description: The forest here is a unique mix of younger, smaller trees and older, sturdy trees, split to the east and the west. The younger trees to the east allow you to see the curve of a trail and a large hill beyond it. The older forest stretches on in every other direction.

e.to_room_name: Before the Slopes of a Hill

69270.name: The Forest Path
69270.description: A dirt path through the forest gives a clear route through the trees. A large hill rises to the south east and the mountains rear up to the north.

69200.name: Forest Trail
69200.description: The trail is straight here, leading deeper into the forest west and back out east.

69201.name: Following the Trail
69201.description: A well-worn trail meanders through the thick pines here, leading to the east and to the west. To the west, the trees are more sparse and young as you approach a large hill, and to the east, the forest continues.

69202.name: Skirting the Hill
69202.description: A large hill rises to the west, most likely a foothill for the mountain range rising to the north. The path bends to the south here to avoid the rougher terrain. The forest around you appears much younger, as though the trees here began their life much later than their sires to the east.

69203.name: Skirting the Hill
69203.description: The path continues on its way around the large hill to the northwest. The rocky terrain is broken and uneven and would be a much more difficult hike than that on the trail.

69204.name: South of the Hill
69204.description: Just to the north rises a large rocky hill, possibly a foothill for the larger mountains to the north. The trail continues to the east and to the west.

69205.name: Skirting the Hill
69205.description: A large hill rises to the northeast. The trees around you still appear to be much younger than you would expect, considering that the trees to the west and further to the south are much larger and older.

nw.to_room_name: Before the Slopes of a Hill

69206.name: Before the Slopes of a Hill
69206.description: A large foothill rises to the east, indicating the start of the mountains to the north. The rocky, steep terrain makes it impossible to proceed east, but the main trail continues to the southeast and the west.

69232.name: Hilltop Forest
69232.description: As you continue to walk along the tree covered hilltop, you notice that some trees appear much younger than others. Looking to the north, you see towering pines and firs and yet, to the south, the trees obviously grew much later.

69233.name: Forested Hill
69233.description: Standing on the side of the hill, near the top, you find yourself on a slight incline as the slope ends its climb to the south and rolls down towards more hills and the mountains to the south. The forest around you is thick with old pine and fir.

69234.name: Forested Trail
69234.description: A faint trail meanders over the forested hillside, beginning to be reclaimed by nature. The trail continues both to the southeast, where it climbs the side of the hill, and to the northwest, leading towards the hill bottom. The trees all around you are thick, but appear younger up the hillside to the south.

69235.name: Forested Trail
69235.description: The trail continues its journey through the forest here, climbing up the hill to the southeast and to the bottom, towards more hills to the north.

69236.name: Northern Side of the Hill
69236.description: The trail here comes up to the base of a large hill towering to the south. To the west, you are not able to make out much more than the towering pines and firs that surround you on all sides.

69237.name: Trail Through the Pines
69237.description: Continuing along the trail, you can't help but feel small and insignificant next to the immense, old trees all around you. The trail leads east and west, both directions leading deeper into the trees.

69238.name: A Crossroads
69238.description: You have come upon a crossroads, where the trail splits in four different directions. To the south and east, the trail wraps around a large hill towering to the southwest. To the north, the trails leads closer to the mountains, and to the west, the trail appears to lead deeper into the forest.

69239.name: Forested Trail
69239.description: The trail here leads both north and south into the forest. To the east, you see a large hill and to the west, nothing but deep forest as far as you can see.

s.to_room_name: Before the Slopes of a Hill

69240.name: Forested Trail
69240.description: Continuing its journey through the forest, the trail passes through the young trees on all sides. Looking further west, you can make out larger trees growing over the smaller, younger trees. To the east you see a large hill rising.

69244.name: Old Forested Path
69244.description: Worn by years of weather and travel, a path leads through the pines and firs. The occasional broken branch lines the path; it appears as though they were snapped off intentionally when passed.

69245.name: Creek Through the Trees
69245.description: A crudely made, wooden bridge spans a wide creek that comes cascading in from the north, disappearing into the pines to the south. Large, moss covered rocks dot the creek bed, breaking up the waters flow and creating white foamed rapids. It would be trecherous to try to cross without the bridge.

69246.name: Crossing the Bridge
69246.description: A wide bridge of stout pine and oaken planks spans a wide creek that bubbles and froths below your feet. Thick, crudely carved railings span the length of the bridge and provide a semblance of safety as you quickly cross to the opposite bank. As you pass the center, you note some faint markings in the sturdy wood.

69247.name: West Bank of the Creek
69247.description: A broad creek carves its way through the forest, leading from the mountains to the north and disappearing to the south. A wooden bridge offers a safe place to ford.
